extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters by direction: I counted only the “Against” rows, excluding all “By” rows. Criminal proceedings against the company, directors, promoters and subsidiaries are Nil, 1 and 1 respectively, for 2 total; statutory/regulatory proceedings against the company and subsidiaries are Nil and 1, for 1 total; tax proceedings against the company and subsidiaries are 3 and 5, for 8 total, excluding promoter/director personal tax matters. The quantified aggregate amount against the company and subsidiaries is the stated against-row total of Rs. 4.17 million plus Rs. 57.13 million = Rs. 61.30 million, or Rs. 6.13 crore; the contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated Total of Rs. 252.85 million as at December 31, 2021, converted to Rs. 25.285 crore. The concentration disclosure is for Fiscal 2021, a full fiscal year, and states that the top five AMCs accounted for 50.77% of total revenue from operations; it is company-wide rather than limited to a business segment. The source tables use millions of rupees, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (Rs. 61.30 million ÷ 10 = Rs. 6.13 crore; Rs. 252.85 million ÷ 10 = Rs. 25.285 crore).
